name: Pommes Anna
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Heat an 8-inch cast iron pan or non-stick skillet with a cup of clarified butter.
3. Using a mandoline (Japanese or German), slice half the potatoes into very thin rounds, drying the potatoes with a clean cloth first.
4. Starting from the center of the pan, overlapping slightly, place slices in a spiral going clockwise. Repeat another spiral counterclockwise.
5. Sprinkle salt and pepper over the layers. Drizzle with clarified butter.
6. Repeat the process of layering, seasoning, and drizzling until the potato cake is about 2 inches high, slicing more potatoes as needed.
7. Gently shake the pan to prevent sticking, then place in the oven for 20 minutes.
8. Use remaining potatoes and butter in another skillet in the same manner.
9. Test the center of Pommes Anna for tenderness.
10. Remove from the pan, drain on paper towel, and cut into wedges.
Yield: 4 servings
